publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:44:06 UTC.

ead.archdesc:
======= RMM08623Tom Harrisson Central Borneo Documents8623Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections2B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 19:12:15 UTC.Describing Archives: A Content Standard >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Tom Harrisson Central Borneo Documents
Harrisson, Tom, 1911-1976.
8623
2.6 cubic feet. 2.6 cubic feet.
1947-1966. In English. <<<<<<< HEAD 3 ======= 3 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 10015647
Scope and content
The collection consists of documents accumulated while Harrisson worked as Curator of the Sarawak Museum, including maps, photographs, notebooks and papers/manuscripts by Harrisson and research assistants.
Preferred Citation
Tom Harrisson Central Borneo Documents, #8623. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Biographical / Historical
Tom Harrisson was a British polymath. In the course of his life he was an ornithologist, explorer, journalist, broadcaster, soldier, guerrilla, ethnologist, museum curator, archaeologist, documentarian, film-maker, conservationist and writer. After serving in WWII, he became the Curator of the Sarawak Museum from 1947-1966.
Borneo. Indonesia.
10015647 <<<<<<< HEAD Journals and notebooks
Scope and Contents
The journals are titled with the initals of Tom Harrison, the year the journal was created, and the the number of the journal.TH48-1194811
Scope and Contents
Bangsa Apau Kayan. 2 p. Census of the Apau Kayan.TH50195011
Scope and Contents
Manis Manuscript. The original handwritten manuscript is in Malay, in five schoolbooks. The English translation follows the page numbers of the Malay manuscript. The translator is W.U. The first page indicated that the information was gathered in January 1950; for some reason, the documents, initially numbered 50/1 to 5, were renumbered 48/1 to 5. Notes indicated that the informations for vol. 1 and 2 were Lenjau and Jok Used, for vols. 3 and 5, A ban U an Tingang of Long Tikan (died 1954).TH50-1 - 48/1 - Manis195011
Scope and Contents
The origin of the Apau Kayan inhabitants. The origin of the faith in Bungan Malan. About menyat tanah. Old faith bird and others. No longer believe now.TH50-2 - 48/2 - Manis195011
Scope and Contents
Continuation: the doctrine of Jok Apui. The affairs in the village at the present day. About Lenjau Used and his companions who came over to Sarawak.TH50-3 - 48-3195012
Scope and Contents
Kenyah verse; Balan Nyaring story. Uyau Ambing Lian died due to sulkiness. The first coming of proper and orderly government to Sarawak. First coming of government into the Baram. About the intention of Pangeran Muda Susu to invade BerunaiTH50-4 - 48-4195012
Scope and Contents
[Cont.] Tuan Aut and Pangulu Tama Bulan The Kalabits were subdued. The Ken yah of the upper Rejang run away. Batang Kayan Kenyah made a war in revenge to those in the Selaan.TH50-5 - 48-5195012
Scope and Contents
[Cont.] The death of Tam a Bulan and the Berunai people's decision to shift the Kenyahs. (The Kenyah run away to the Kayan river). (The people of Brunei attack the Baram; they are driven away by the Rajah). 20 N.B. The titles between brackets are in the Malay text, but not the translation.TH50-61950-06-2213
Scope and Contents
Population of Long Buroi Sebop. 6 p., handwritten .TH58-21958-12-1213
Scope and Contents
TH, Penghulu Apoi Injau of Long Kemuat, Bahau river (revised work for research assistant) , 1 p .TH58-3195813
Scope and Contents
TH with Penghulu Apoi Injou. Handwritten exercise book, 31 p. Almost illegible.TH58-419581
Scope and Contents
The Bahau under Penghulu Apoi Injou by Tom Harrisson. Schoolbook, handwritten by TH, 10 p.TH58-1 - KEN 1958 SERIES195814
Scope and Contents
1. Meaning of term "Bahau" and all its uses 2. Present tribal (group) organisation of Indonesian Kenyahs 3. Relation of Indonesian to Sarawak Kenyah 4. Relations of Kenyah and Kayan 5. Relation between Kenyah and Saban of Ulu Bahau 6. Relation of Kenyahs and Badangs 7. Origin stories and myths of Kenyahs 8. Full story of Kenyah migration 9. The Kenyah groups and marriage laws 10. Punan stories and relations with the Kenyahs 11 . Stones and Kudis 12. Mr. Harrisson's notes on Kenyah class structure [In manuscript form , typed by J .R.] 13. Funeral rites (by class) 14. Genealogies, by Temenggong Lawai Jau, M.B.E., Penghulu Liim Bilong and Penghulu Apoi Injou195814
Scope and Contents
Ken Exercise books 2/58 - 14/58, 10/58 missingTH59-1 Ken Series195915
Scope and Contents
1. Origin stories and myths of the Kenyah Transcribed by J.R., 1 p. 2. Full accounts of Kenyah migrations. 6 p . 3. Relation of Indonesia to Sarawak Kenyah , 1 p. Transcribed by J .R. 4. How the Kenyah got split into groups and what these group terms mean and how they work standard marriage laws and funeral rites by class. 18 p. 5. Relations of Kenyahs and Kayans and how they originated differently , etc., 3 p. Transcribed by J .R. 6. Relations of Kenyah and Sabans (Ulu Bahau) , 1 p. 7. Relations of Kenyahs and Badangs by Pa Bit Enjok . 3 p. 8. Relations of Kenyahs and Punans. Not available ; see note above. 9. Present distribution and numbers of Indonesian Punans. Not available ; see note above . 10. All known Ken yah stories especially about historical stones and kudi ; L. Balau (cave burial) [by TH] ; Gan Noyang [by TH]; Ancient food of human being; Ad at Lamimpa; Adat anak or "Notong pusha" . Only the last two sections have been transcribed; the schoolbook has 31 p. 11. Divisions of movable properties by the Ken yah. 4 p . 12. How are the seasons, years (calendar) worked by Kenyah. 3 p. 13. Who lived before the Kenyahs and Kayans in their respective countries before them . The book contains 3 p. by Sandin and 12 by TH. Transcribed by J .R . 14. Kenyah/Kayan contact with N.E.I. Government. 7 p. 15. How Christianity and Bungan religion come to Kenyah? 6 p . 16. Head hunter war. 6 p . 17. Kenyah/Kayan simple vocabulary. Handwritten . 5 p . plus notes byTH.TH59-21959-01-2616
Scope and Contents
Pa Bit's comments on various exhibits in the Museum. 7 p. (also in handwritten form, 13 p., schoolbook) .Th59-31959-01-1616
Scope and Contents
Letter of Sandin to TH. 2 p . about KEN 1959 series.TH59-41959-04-0316
Scope and Contents
Temenggong Oyong Lawai Jau to TH. Handwritten original in Malay and translation ; on bark coats .1959-1016
Scope and Contents
Ken - 10/59 Adat LamimpaTH60-11960-06-0116
Scope and Contents
Mamat. Exercise book in TH's hand. Approximately 50 p.TH60-21960-1116
Scope and Contents
Three Leppo Tau punishments, by Pa Bit Enjok and TH, 4 p.TH60-31960-06-161
Scope and Contents
Dyak tribe migrates. Copied from Times of Indonesia, vol. 23, no. 45, p. 4, 1 p.TH60-41960-12-051
Scope and Contents
Belaga revisited 1932-1960. Handwritten by TH. Names of pupils in Abun Matu school, Belaga: than every races [sic], 22 II 60, 1 p.TH61-1196117
Scope and Contents
LNK 1961 SERIES The original notebooks are not available (with exceptions noted below) . The documents are numbered LNK/1/61 to LNK/8/61. There is no LNK/7 /61. 'LNK' probably stands for 'Long Nawang Kenyah'. 1. The origin of the Kenyah; the origin of the Mamat feast. The order of dress; the birding cult. 22 p. 2. The Nutong Pusha feast or a feast for naming the child. Includes: the Nutong Pusha feast. The farming rules and regulations. Planting of padi. The weeding season. The harvest. 7 p. 3. The life of the Kenyah from birth to death. Contents: Birth; boyhood; bachelorhood; marriage; sick (Uyan Udip); sick (Dayong); death and burial. 21 p. 4. The building of the new long-house . 6 p. 5. Muko mending (a ceremony held for driving away diseases). 6 p. 6. The Kenyah of Borneo, 5 p. by Pa Uloi Apoi, assisted byPa Poi Anye, 64 years old. 7. [not available] 8. Mamat dress check list. The order of mamat dress.TH61-1a196118
Scope and Contents
Exercise book "Marp.at dress" 10 p., handwritten by Sandin, with notes by TH.TH61-1b196118
Scope and Contents
Exercise book "the order ofMamat dress, book 2", with 10 pages of drawings.TH61-21961-01-0218
Scope and Contents
Tena Payau statue by Pa Tuloi Bit, assisted by Pa Poi Anye and Pa Jalong Bilong, with date handwritten by TH, 1/2/61, and note LNK/1/61, although link to LNK series is not clear.TH61-31961-01-2718
Scope and Contents
TH Two Orang stories from Central Borneo. 5 p., handwritten. On Kayan and Punan.TH61-41961-02-0418
Scope and Contents
The Kenyah songs and prayers recorded by B. S[ an din] at Bungaiow annex. Handwritten, 3 p., in schoolbook.TH61-51961-02-1818
Scope and Contents
TH The Hornbill, 2 p., handwritten by TH.TH61-61961-0218
Scope and Contents
TH Leppo Tau. Handwritten by TH, 23 p. Exercise bookTH61-71961-03-1919
Scope and Contents
TH Long Nawang: "Tree of life" ; omens; mamat and hombill; dayong; masks and bark coats; the Pua people: megalithic: ulu Baloi. 11 p. Also manuscript in TH's hand, 14 p.TH61-8192619
Scope and Contents
Translation from J.M. Elshout, 1926. "De Kenja-Dajaks uit het Apo-Kajangebied", Chapter III, section VI on Mamat rituals. 88 p. [This may have been translated by Frans Baartmans at Galvin's request].TH-91961-03-2019
Scope and Contents
TH TH discussing Elshout translation with Pa' Tuloi. 6 p.TH61-101961-03-2119
Scope and Contents
TH Separate head houses. 1 p.TH61-111961-03-2219
Scope and Contents
TH Note on Keraman. 3 p.TH61-121961-03-2319
Scope and Contents
TH Batu Kawi etc. -- Adat siap. 2 p.TH61-131961-03-2419
Scope and Contents
TH Tree of life in sickness ceremony. 1 p.TH61-141961-03-2519
Scope and Contents
TH Tecknonyms (Pa Tuloi) 1 p.TH61-15196119
Scope and Contents
Added note on Belawing. 1 p.TH61-16196119
Scope and Contents
Predecessor to Bungan Cult and birdsTH61-171961-06-23110
Scope and Contents
Letter of Raymond Rudes to TH. about visit to Long Sa'anTH61-181961110
Scope and Contents
Terselah lama bangsa Kayan, T.K. Baling . Foolscap-size hardbound notebook, with 80 p. of handwritten notes in Malay. The bulk of it was written by someone else than Baling, but there are a few notes in Baling's handwriting. There is no date, but it was written in or before 1961 , as it seems to have served as a basis. for the Belaga Book (see below) . Pp. 27-28 have been torn out, and a note on p. 26 says "Start from p. 29"; p. 1-26 have been glued together; there is a partial overlap between the two parts. The various sections are numbered in Malay rather than ciphers, i.e. "bagian ke-dua", "bagian ke-tiga", etc . These have been replaced by ciphers below for convenience .TH61-191961-11-07-1961-11-10110
Scope and Contents
BN111961TH61-201961110
Scope and Contents
With a note in TH's hand , " Preliminary". This will be referred tohere as BA/111961ATH61-211961-11-01111
Scope and Contents
BA/111961 With a note in TH's hand , "Out of date" . This will be referred to here as BA/1119618 .TH61-221961-11-11-1961-11-15111
Scope and Contents
BA/211961TH61-231961-11-01-1961-11-02111
Scope and Contents
BN2/1961TH61-241961-11-02-1961-11-03111
Scope and Contents
BA/3/1961 With a note in TH's hand, "Out of date."TH61-251961-11-15111
Scope and Contents
BN4/1961TH61-261961-10112
Scope and Contents
TK Baleng, Kayan , B. Sandin. With a note in TH's hand, "Preliminary analysis of ... 20/10/61".This will be referred to as [BN5/1961].TH61-281961113
Scope and Contents
BELAGABOOK - See Journal for titles. The following description of the Belaga Book indicates between brackets for each section the relevant handwritten documents ; in some cases, more than one version is available. The section numbers have been added by me.TH61-291961113
Scope and Contents
Adat adat bangsa Kayan. By Baling. Typewritten in Kayan by the author. 61 p. n.d., apparently writtel). in 1961. The first page (not paginated, and two pages numbered "24", are in Malay). Pp. 1-23 deal with the traditional Kayan religion, pp. 24-61 with the Bungan religion. I have translated this text into English; I plan to annotate it and publish it with an introduction .TH61-301961113
Scope and Contents
Kayan. Bahasa Malay (T.K. Baleng), dated 27 x 61 by TH. 2 p. in Baling's handwriting, in Malay, torn from a large notebook. The text has then been typed (3 p.) The text is closely related to the beginning of Adat adat bangsa Kayan .TH61-311961113
Scope and Contents
TK Baleng. Bungan religion. 28-31110/61. 85 p. (only oddnumbered pages have text). In Sandin's handwriting. Apparently a translation of Part II of Adat adat bangsa Kayan. This seems to indicate that Baling translated his Kayan text into Malay (see previous document), which was then translated into English.TH61-321961114
Scope and Contents
Mengtahwi adat Bungan Malan Paselong Luan. Adat baru Kayan. 15/11161 by Baling Abun of Uma Aging aged 50 yrs . 26 p. plus 4 p. at beginning, barred off by author. Handwritten in Malay by the author. Transcribed by J.R.TH61-331961-11114
Scope and Contents
Partial Bungan. Baleng . Schoolbook handwritten by Baling, 14 p. Cover page written by TH. With partial pagination, 5 to 10.TH61-341961-03114
Scope and Contents
(1) Partial Bungan. Baleng. 26 p. (paginated 35-47). This is. a continuation of the previous item, also in Baling's handwriting. The pagination suggests that there was another schoolbook.TH61-351961-02114
Scope and Contents
BELAGA TH. Kayan graded society + Masks etc. 54 p. Schoolbook in TH's handwriting. Section numbers added by me. 1. General: Kayan cf Leppo Tau 2. The Kayan Ayou' (Aiyow) 3. Masks: Hudp' 4. Tuans & these rites 5. Animism manifest 6. Papan Ta'kap 7. Totemism relics 8. Kayan cf Kajang 9. Jar relic & Meja jar [torn off] 10. Lack of space ... [no text] 11. The horn bill & the dance [no text] 12. Kin terminology & name use 13. B1rd Omens [no text] 14. Cave Burial! 15. Leadership weaker than Kenyah 16. Batu Keling 17. Kayan ... 18. Teknonymy 19. Divide 20. Aki Dian 21. Ma Pun 22. 23. BersumaTH61-361961-02-14-1961-02-16114
Scope and Contents
Belaga. Admin I. Handwritten by TH.TH61-371961-02-17114
Scope and Contents
Belaga. Admin II. Handwritten by TH.TH61-381961-02114
Scope and Contents
Sec. Burial I. Handwritten by TH.TH61-391961-03-01115
Scope and Contents
Sec. Burial II. Kelirengs Belaga. Handwritten by TH.TH61-401961-02-25115
Scope and Contents
General Belagalore survey report. By Benedict Sandin, 25 II 61. 9 p.TH61-411961-03-02115
Scope and Contents
Kayan shamanism. TH 2/3/61. Approx. 10-15 p. Handwritten by !H. with typewritten note, apparently by Baling, discussing a papan.TH61-421961-02-19-1961-02-20115
Scope and Contents
Aki Lengio, G.O.M. of the Western Kayans. Approx. 30 p. Handwritten by TH.TH61-431961-02-22115
Scope and Contents
TH Some geographic factors on Borneo's interior life. Approx. 25 p. Handwritten.TH61-441961-02-24115
Scope and Contents
TH War debt: help others. 3 p. Handwritten by TH. About a Belaga Ukit.TH61-451961-02-2621
Scope and Contents
T.K. Baleng/TH Kayans in Apo Kayan, Belungan and elsewhere. 2 p. by TH. 2 p. by Baling. Handwritten.TH61-461961-02-1961-0321
Scope and Contents
Puso's Tang jar, 6 p. Handwritten by TH.TH61-47196121
Scope and Contents
Ini semua lis nama2 orang yang berkerja S.R.D. menulong perintah, pada 1.6.1945. Written by Baling Avun in 1961 or later.TH61-481961-11-2021
Scope and Contents
Kapuas/111961. 105 p.TH61-491961-11-24-1961-11-2621
Scope and Contents
Kapuas/2/1961. 83 p.TH61-501961-11-2121
Scope and Contents
Kapuas/3/1961, 65 p.TH61-511961-11-2722
Scope and Contents
Kapuas/4/1961. 11 p.TH62-521961-11-1722
Scope and Contents
Kapuas/6/1961. 7 p. For some reason this is referred to as Book 5 in TH61-57.TH61-531961-11-1822
Scope and Contents
Kapuas BK 5/61. 35 p. For some reason this is referred to as Book 6 in TH61-57. On the first page, this book has the following note: "This note book is for the Kayan map drawn by Baling A bun".TH61-54196122
Scope and Contents
Kapuas/7/61. Not located, may not exist.TH61-551961-11-2422
Scope and Contents
Kapuas/8/61, Kayan folktales. 51 p. Not typed. The text is Malay. It contains three stories: 1. Cherita lima orang adek beradek dengan bantu, pp. 3-23. 2. Cherita Jo Jok Bam, pp. 25-35 3. Hanyang Ketong, pp. 37-51 The informant is Dulah Usat, age 30, Kayan from Uma Suling in Kapuas.TH61-56196122
Scope and Contents
Kapuas/9/61, Kayan folktales. The text is in Malay. As the previous item, it is in Sandin's handwriting, and is not part of the typed Kapuas book. It contains three stories: 1. [end of story 3 above], p. 3-17. 2. Cherita Lelabi, p. 19-25. 3. Cherita Hila dengan burongjeruit, p. 27-45. The informant for story 2 is Deng Mering, 58 years old, a Kayan from Uma Suling in the Kapuas; Dulah Usat provided the other story. These stories were recorded in Kuching.TH61-56a1961-11-2722
Scope and Contents
Transcript of Mengie song, Kapuas tape 5. 25 p., handwritten by TH and others. Informant Dullah Uvat [sic]. Despite the absence of corresponding number, constitutes the lOth notebook in the Kapuas 1961 series.TH61-57196122
Scope and Contents
KAPUASBOOK This is a 57 p. typed text in English. It is a transcription ofTH61- 48 to TH61-53. The Kapuas book calls Kapuas/6/1961 "Kapuas book 5", and Kapuas BK 5/61 "Kapuas book 6". The informants were Dian Ndjuk, then 62 years old, Ding Aving, 51 years old, and Dollah Udin, 32 years old, all three Kapuas Kayan. In addition, Baling provided some information. Section numbers have been added by me.TH61-581961-10-3022
Scope and Contents
Tersebut nama rumah 2 p. Handwritten by BalingTH61-59196122TH61-60196122
Scope and Contents
KY/1/7 Names of Kayan villages in Kapuas. 2 p. HandwrittenTH61-611961-11-2623
Scope and Contents
TH with penghule Dian Ndjok, Mah Aging , schoolbook 23 p. Handwritten notes by TH on masksTH62-11962-11-1523

Comment on Mr. Kooijman's draft. 7 p. By THTH63-1196323
Scope and Contents
The various peoples of Kalirnantan Barat. by Alexander Kandaran. 4p. "Vari o us·· has been barred and replaced by ""native"' and ··by Alexander Kaodaran·· has been barred. '" BsrrH/63-· ha> been added at the e nd.TH64-1196423

Megaliths of the Apo Kayan, by Ray Rudes. I. Data Dian burial tomb. II . Data Dian statue of the woman sloping[?] a dog or a hog! III. Long Djeng and Long Lui (Kajan lot) stone stools and 'grinders'. 5 p.TH66-11966-05-0925

TH Mamat paper, addendum. 1 p.TH66-21966-06-2225

TH Note on Mamat. Discussion with Jalong, Leppo Tau of Long Nawang, on basis of his pictures representing sohun grades at mamat. 2 p.TH66-31966-10-2425

TH Mamat pictures by Jalong: commentary. 12 p.TH66-41966-03-0325

A note on "Laya", by Galvin. 2 p. On the Murik's view of swiftlets. A comment on Galvin [1408].TH66-51966-1025

Omen birds, by Jalong Liban. 2 p.TH67-11967-01-1826
Scope and Contents
Kenyah marriage customs and adaptations. By Galvin. 8 p., plus Appendix: "Ken yah text", 2 p. With handwritten note, " not. for ~ublication" . Discusses the adaptation of Ken yah marnage ntuals to Roman Catholicism.TH n.d. 1Undated26

[Notes by TH on Mamat]. Hard cover notebook. 41 p .TH n.d. 3Undated26

Scope and ContentsThe last of the Serus. A study in dynami<: decay, by TH, 81p. [incomplete; written after 1949 and before 1966] . I. Background A. The last 500 years II. Extinction-- Submergence III. The last of the Serus IV. The semi and ex-Serus V . Origin and expansion of the Serus VI. Mount Tebujang VII . The downward chainTH n.d. 12Undated28

B/W pictures of bark cloth; Sarawak Nos. 67, 69, 71, 72, 74, 76, 78, 80, 82, 84, 86, 88, 90, 92, 94, 97, 103, 104, 105, 106, 108, 110, 112, 113, 114, 115, 116, 117, 118, 119, 120, 121, 122, 123, 124, 126, 127PhotographsUndated210

Scope and Contents
unlabeledPhotographsUndated211
Scope and Contents
BW pictures; Headgear with Hornbill Rhinoplax; Nos. 35, 36, 37, 38, 39, 40PhotographsUndated211
Scope and Contents
Dayak shield, F. Ling Roth vol 2:129; correspondence with J.F. Johnston, V. of Manchester, Mary Fregear, AshmoleanPhotographsUndated211
Scope and Contents
Marber Bond (?)Correspondence1962-1966212Documents1959-1960213Documents1961-1964214Documents1958215Documents1964216Documents1962-1963217Drawings1960218 ======= Journals and notebooks
Scope and Contents
The journals are titled with the initals of Tom Harrison, the year the journal was created, and the the number of the journal.TH48-1194811
Scope and Contents
Bangsa Apau Kayan. 2 p. Census of the Apau Kayan.TH50195011
Scope and Contents
Manis Manuscript. The original handwritten manuscript is in Malay, in five schoolbooks. The English translation follows the page numbers of the Malay manuscript. The translator is W.U. The first page indicated that the information was gathered in January 1950; for some reason, the documents, initially numbered 50/1 to 5, were renumbered 48/1 to 5. Notes indicated that the informations for vol. 1 and 2 were Lenjau and Jok Used, for vols. 3 and 5, A ban U an Tingang of Long Tikan (died 1954).TH50-1 - 48/1 - Manis195011
Scope and Contents
The origin of the Apau Kayan inhabitants. The origin of the faith in Bungan Malan. About menyat tanah. Old faith bird and others. No longer believe now.TH50-2 - 48/2 - Manis195011
Scope and Contents
Continuation: the doctrine of Jok Apui. The affairs in the village at the present day. About Lenjau Used and his companions who came over to Sarawak.TH50-3 - 48-3195012
Scope and Contents
Kenyah verse; Balan Nyaring story. Uyau Ambing Lian died due to sulkiness. The first coming of proper and orderly government to Sarawak. First coming of government into the Baram. About the intention of Pangeran Muda Susu to invade BerunaiTH50-4 - 48-4195012
Scope and Contents
[Cont.] Tuan Aut and Pangulu Tama Bulan The Kalabits were subdued. The Ken yah of the upper Rejang run away. Batang Kayan Kenyah made a war in revenge to those in the Selaan.TH50-5 - 48-5195012
Scope and Contents
[Cont.] The death of Tam a Bulan and the Berunai people's decision to shift the Kenyahs. (The Kenyah run away to the Kayan river). (The people of Brunei attack the Baram; they are driven away by the Rajah). 20 N.B. The titles between brackets are in the Malay text, but not the translation.TH50-61950-06-2213
Scope and Contents
Population of Long Buroi Sebop. 6 p., handwritten .TH58-21958-12-1213
Scope and Contents
TH, Penghulu Apoi Injau of Long Kemuat, Bahau river (revised work for research assistant) , 1 p .TH58-3195813
Scope and Contents
TH with Penghulu Apoi Injou. Handwritten exercise book, 31 p. Almost illegible.TH58-419581
Scope and Contents
The Bahau under Penghulu Apoi Injou by Tom Harrisson. Schoolbook, handwritten by TH, 10 p.TH58-1 - KEN 1958 SERIES195814
Scope and Contents
1. Meaning of term "Bahau" and all its uses 2. Present tribal (group) organisation of Indonesian Kenyahs 3. Relation of Indonesian to Sarawak Kenyah 4. Relations of Kenyah and Kayan 5. Relation between Kenyah and Saban of Ulu Bahau 6. Relation of Kenyahs and Badangs 7. Origin stories and myths of Kenyahs 8. Full story of Kenyah migration 9. The Kenyah groups and marriage laws 10. Punan stories and relations with the Kenyahs 11 . Stones and Kudis 12. Mr. Harrisson's notes on Kenyah class structure [In manuscript form , typed by J .R.] 13. Funeral rites (by class) 14. Genealogies, by Temenggong Lawai Jau, M.B.E., Penghulu Liim Bilong and Penghulu Apoi Injou195814
Scope and Contents
Ken Exercise books 2/58 - 14/58, 10/58 missingTH59-1 Ken Series195915
Scope and Contents
1. Origin stories and myths of the Kenyah Transcribed by J.R., 1 p. 2. Full accounts of Kenyah migrations. 6 p . 3. Relation of Indonesia to Sarawak Kenyah , 1 p. Transcribed by J .R. 4. How the Kenyah got split into groups and what these group terms mean and how they work standard marriage laws and funeral rites by class. 18 p. 5. Relations of Kenyahs and Kayans and how they originated differently , etc., 3 p. Transcribed by J .R. 6. Relations of Kenyah and Sabans (Ulu Bahau) , 1 p. 7. Relations of Kenyahs and Badangs by Pa Bit Enjok . 3 p. 8. Relations of Kenyahs and Punans. Not available ; see note above. 9. Present distribution and numbers of Indonesian Punans. Not available ; see note above . 10. All known Ken yah stories especially about historical stones and kudi ; L. Balau (cave burial) [by TH] ; Gan Noyang [by TH]; Ancient food of human being; Ad at Lamimpa; Adat anak or "Notong pusha" . Only the last two sections have been transcribed; the schoolbook has 31 p. 11. Divisions of movable properties by the Ken yah. 4 p . 12. How are the seasons, years (calendar) worked by Kenyah. 3 p. 13. Who lived before the Kenyahs and Kayans in their respective countries before them . The book contains 3 p. by Sandin and 12 by TH. Transcribed by J .R . 14. Kenyah/Kayan contact with N.E.I. Government. 7 p. 15. How Christianity and Bungan religion come to Kenyah? 6 p . 16. Head hunter war. 6 p . 17. Kenyah/Kayan simple vocabulary. Handwritten . 5 p . plus notes byTH.TH59-21959-01-2616
Scope and Contents
Pa Bit's comments on various exhibits in the Museum. 7 p. (also in handwritten form, 13 p., schoolbook) .Th59-31959-01-1616
Scope and Contents
Letter of Sandin to TH. 2 p . about KEN 1959 series.TH59-41959-04-0316
Scope and Contents
Temenggong Oyong Lawai Jau to TH. Handwritten original in Malay and translation ; on bark coats .1959-1016
Scope and Contents
Ken - 10/59 Adat LamimpaTH60-11960-06-0116
Scope and Contents
Mamat. Exercise book in TH's hand. Approximately 50 p.TH60-21960-1116
Scope and Contents
Three Leppo Tau punishments, by Pa Bit Enjok and TH, 4 p.TH60-31960-06-161
Scope and Contents
Dyak tribe migrates. Copied from Times of Indonesia, vol. 23, no. 45, p. 4, 1 p.TH60-41960-12-051
Scope and Contents
Belaga revisited 1932-1960. Handwritten by TH. Names of pupils in Abun Matu school, Belaga: than every races [sic], 22 II 60, 1 p.TH61-1196117
Scope and Contents
LNK 1961 SERIES The original notebooks are not available (with exceptions noted below) . The documents are numbered LNK/1/61 to LNK/8/61. There is no LNK/7 /61. 'LNK' probably stands for 'Long Nawang Kenyah'. 1. The origin of the Kenyah; the origin of the Mamat feast. The order of dress; the birding cult. 22 p. 2. The Nutong Pusha feast or a feast for naming the child. Includes: the Nutong Pusha feast. The farming rules and regulations. Planting of padi. The weeding season. The harvest. 7 p. 3. The life of the Kenyah from birth to death. Contents: Birth; boyhood; bachelorhood; marriage; sick (Uyan Udip); sick (Dayong); death and burial. 21 p. 4. The building of the new long-house . 6 p. 5. Muko mending (a ceremony held for driving away diseases). 6 p. 6. The Kenyah of Borneo, 5 p. by Pa Uloi Apoi, assisted byPa Poi Anye, 64 years old. 7. [not available] 8. Mamat dress check list. The order of mamat dress.TH61-1a196118
Scope and Contents
Exercise book "Marp.at dress" 10 p., handwritten by Sandin, with notes by TH.TH61-1b196118
Scope and Contents
Exercise book "the order ofMamat dress, book 2", with 10 pages of drawings.TH61-21961-01-0218
Scope and Contents
Tena Payau statue by Pa Tuloi Bit, assisted by Pa Poi Anye and Pa Jalong Bilong, with date handwritten by TH, 1/2/61, and note LNK/1/61, although link to LNK series is not clear.TH61-31961-01-2718
Scope and Contents
TH Two Orang stories from Central Borneo. 5 p., handwritten. On Kayan and Punan.TH61-41961-02-0418
Scope and Contents
The Kenyah songs and prayers recorded by B. S[ an din] at Bungaiow annex. Handwritten, 3 p., in schoolbook.TH61-51961-02-1818
Scope and Contents
TH The Hornbill, 2 p., handwritten by TH.TH61-61961-0218
Scope and Contents
TH Leppo Tau. Handwritten by TH, 23 p. Exercise bookTH61-71961-03-1919
Scope and Contents
TH Long Nawang: "Tree of life" ; omens; mamat and hombill; dayong; masks and bark coats; the Pua people: megalithic: ulu Baloi. 11 p. Also manuscript in TH's hand, 14 p.TH61-8192619
Scope and Contents
Translation from J.M. Elshout, 1926. "De Kenja-Dajaks uit het Apo-Kajangebied", Chapter III, section VI on Mamat rituals. 88 p. [This may have been translated by Frans Baartmans at Galvin's request].TH-91961-03-2019
Scope and Contents
TH TH discussing Elshout translation with Pa' Tuloi. 6 p.TH61-101961-03-2119
Scope and Contents
TH Separate head houses. 1 p.TH61-111961-03-2219
Scope and Contents
TH Note on Keraman. 3 p.TH61-121961-03-2319
Scope and Contents
TH Batu Kawi etc. -- Adat siap. 2 p.TH61-131961-03-2419
Scope and Contents
TH Tree of life in sickness ceremony. 1 p.TH61-141961-03-2519
Scope and Contents
TH Tecknonyms (Pa Tuloi) 1 p.TH61-15196119
Scope and Contents
Added note on Belawing. 1 p.TH61-16196119
Scope and Contents
Predecessor to Bungan Cult and birdsTH61-171961-06-23110
Scope and Contents
Letter of Raymond Rudes to TH. about visit to Long Sa'anTH61-181961110
Scope and Contents
Terselah lama bangsa Kayan, T.K. Baling . Foolscap-size hardbound notebook, with 80 p. of handwritten notes in Malay. The bulk of it was written by someone else than Baling, but there are a few notes in Baling's handwriting. There is no date, but it was written in or before 1961 , as it seems to have served as a basis. for the Belaga Book (see below) . Pp. 27-28 have been torn out, and a note on p. 26 says "Start from p. 29"; p. 1-26 have been glued together; there is a partial overlap between the two parts. The various sections are numbered in Malay rather than ciphers, i.e. "bagian ke-dua", "bagian ke-tiga", etc . These have been replaced by ciphers below for convenience .TH61-191961-11-07-1961-11-10110
Scope and Contents
BN111961TH61-201961110
Scope and Contents
With a note in TH's hand , " Preliminary". This will be referred tohere as BA/111961ATH61-211961-11-01111
Scope and Contents
BA/111961 With a note in TH's hand , "Out of date" . This will be referred to here as BA/1119618 .TH61-221961-11-11-1961-11-15111
Scope and Contents
BA/211961TH61-231961-11-01-1961-11-02111
Scope and Contents
BN2/1961TH61-241961-11-02-1961-11-03111
Scope and Contents
BA/3/1961 With a note in TH's hand, "Out of date."TH61-251961-11-15111
Scope and Contents
BN4/1961TH61-261961-10112
Scope and Contents
TK Baleng, Kayan , B. Sandin. With a note in TH's hand, "Preliminary analysis of ... 20/10/61".This will be referred to as [BN5/1961].TH61-281961113
Scope and Contents
BELAGABOOK - See Journal for titles. The following description of the Belaga Book indicates between brackets for each section the relevant handwritten documents ; in some cases, more than one version is available. The section numbers have been added by me.TH61-291961113
Scope and Contents
Adat adat bangsa Kayan. By Baling. Typewritten in Kayan by the author. 61 p. n.d., apparently writtel). in 1961. The first page (not paginated, and two pages numbered "24", are in Malay). Pp. 1-23 deal with the traditional Kayan religion, pp. 24-61 with the Bungan religion. I have translated this text into English; I plan to annotate it and publish it with an introduction .TH61-301961113
Scope and Contents
Kayan. Bahasa Malay (T.K. Baleng), dated 27 x 61 by TH. 2 p. in Baling's handwriting, in Malay, torn from a large notebook. The text has then been typed (3 p.) The text is closely related to the beginning of Adat adat bangsa Kayan .TH61-311961113
Scope and Contents
TK Baleng. Bungan religion. 28-31110/61. 85 p. (only oddnumbered pages have text). In Sandin's handwriting. Apparently a translation of Part II of Adat adat bangsa Kayan. This seems to indicate that Baling translated his Kayan text into Malay (see previous document), which was then translated into English.TH61-321961114
Scope and Contents
Mengtahwi adat Bungan Malan Paselong Luan. Adat baru Kayan. 15/11161 by Baling Abun of Uma Aging aged 50 yrs . 26 p. plus 4 p. at beginning, barred off by author. Handwritten in Malay by the author. Transcribed by J.R.TH61-331961-11114
Scope and Contents
Partial Bungan. Baleng . Schoolbook handwritten by Baling, 14 p. Cover page written by TH. With partial pagination, 5 to 10.TH61-341961-03114
Scope and Contents
(1) Partial Bungan. Baleng. 26 p. (paginated 35-47). This is. a continuation of the previous item, also in Baling's handwriting. The pagination suggests that there was another schoolbook.TH61-351961-02114
Scope and Contents
BELAGA TH. Kayan graded society + Masks etc. 54 p. Schoolbook in TH's handwriting. Section numbers added by me. 1. General: Kayan cf Leppo Tau 2. The Kayan Ayou' (Aiyow) 3. Masks: Hudp' 4. Tuans & these rites 5. Animism manifest 6. Papan Ta'kap 7. Totemism relics 8. Kayan cf Kajang 9. Jar relic & Meja jar [torn off] 10. Lack of space ... [no text] 11. The horn bill & the dance [no text] 12. Kin terminology & name use 13. B1rd Omens [no text] 14. Cave Burial! 15. Leadership weaker than Kenyah 16. Batu Keling 17. Kayan ... 18. Teknonymy 19. Divide 20. Aki Dian 21. Ma Pun 22. 23. BersumaTH61-361961-02-14-1961-02-16114
Scope and Contents
Belaga. Admin I. Handwritten by TH.TH61-371961-02-17114
Scope and Contents
Belaga. Admin II. Handwritten by TH.TH61-381961-02114
Scope and Contents
Sec. Burial I. Handwritten by TH.TH61-391961-03-01115
Scope and Contents
Sec. Burial II. Kelirengs Belaga. Handwritten by TH.TH61-401961-02-25115
Scope and Contents
General Belagalore survey report. By Benedict Sandin, 25 II 61. 9 p.TH61-411961-03-02115
Scope and Contents
Kayan shamanism. TH 2/3/61. Approx. 10-15 p. Handwritten by !H. with typewritten note, apparently by Baling, discussing a papan.TH61-421961-02-19-1961-02-20115
Scope and Contents
Aki Lengio, G.O.M. of the Western Kayans. Approx. 30 p. Handwritten by TH.TH61-431961-02-22115
Scope and Contents
TH Some geographic factors on Borneo's interior life. Approx. 25 p. Handwritten.TH61-441961-02-24115
Scope and Contents
TH War debt: help others. 3 p. Handwritten by TH. About a Belaga Ukit.TH61-451961-02-2621
Scope and Contents
T.K. Baleng/TH Kayans in Apo Kayan, Belungan and elsewhere. 2 p. by TH. 2 p. by Baling. Handwritten.TH61-461961-02-1961-0321
Scope and Contents
Puso's Tang jar, 6 p. Handwritten by TH.TH61-47196121
Scope and Contents
Ini semua lis nama2 orang yang berkerja S.R.D. menulong perintah, pada 1.6.1945. Written by Baling Avun in 1961 or later.TH61-481961-11-2021
Scope and Contents
Kapuas/111961. 105 p.TH61-491961-11-24-1961-11-2621
Scope and Contents
Kapuas/2/1961. 83 p.TH61-501961-11-2121
Scope and Contents
Kapuas/3/1961, 65 p.TH61-511961-11-2722
Scope and Contents
Kapuas/4/1961. 11 p.TH62-521961-11-1722
Scope and Contents
Kapuas/6/1961. 7 p. For some reason this is referred to as Book 5 in TH61-57.TH61-531961-11-1822
Scope and Contents
Kapuas BK 5/61. 35 p. For some reason this is referred to as Book 6 in TH61-57. On the first page, this book has the following note: "This note book is for the Kayan map drawn by Baling A bun".TH61-54196122
Scope and Contents
Kapuas/7/61. Not located, may not exist.TH61-551961-11-2422
Scope and Contents
Kapuas/8/61, Kayan folktales. 51 p. Not typed. The text is Malay. It contains three stories: 1. Cherita lima orang adek beradek dengan bantu, pp. 3-23. 2. Cherita Jo Jok Bam, pp. 25-35 3. Hanyang Ketong, pp. 37-51 The informant is Dulah Usat, age 30, Kayan from Uma Suling in Kapuas.TH61-56196122
Scope and Contents
Kapuas/9/61, Kayan folktales. The text is in Malay. As the previous item, it is in Sandin's handwriting, and is not part of the typed Kapuas book. It contains three stories: 1. [end of story 3 above], p. 3-17. 2. Cherita Lelabi, p. 19-25. 3. Cherita Hila dengan burongjeruit, p. 27-45. The informant for story 2 is Deng Mering, 58 years old, a Kayan from Uma Suling in the Kapuas; Dulah Usat provided the other story. These stories were recorded in Kuching.TH61-56a1961-11-2722
Scope and Contents
Transcript of Mengie song, Kapuas tape 5. 25 p., handwritten by TH and others. Informant Dullah Uvat [sic]. Despite the absence of corresponding number, constitutes the lOth notebook in the Kapuas 1961 series.TH61-57196122
Scope and Contents
KAPUASBOOK This is a 57 p. typed text in English. It is a transcription ofTH61- 48 to TH61-53. The Kapuas book calls Kapuas/6/1961 "Kapuas book 5", and Kapuas BK 5/61 "Kapuas book 6". The informants were Dian Ndjuk, then 62 years old, Ding Aving, 51 years old, and Dollah Udin, 32 years old, all three Kapuas Kayan. In addition, Baling provided some information. Section numbers have been added by me.TH61-581961-10-3022
Scope and Contents
Tersebut nama rumah 2 p. Handwritten by BalingTH61-59196122TH61-60196122

KY/1/7 Names of Kayan villages in Kapuas. 2 p. HandwrittenTH61-611961-11-2623
